overview: Ruby Storm is a W/U/R combo deck that accelerates mana with Ruby Medallion and Pyretic Ritual to cast cheap instants and sorceries rapidly. The game plan revolves around building a high storm count and using Ral, Monsoon Mage to replay key spells from the graveyard or resolve a finisher with multiple copies. Success requires protecting early plays from interaction while digging for the exact sequence of accelerants and draw needed to go off turns two through four.
win_conditions: Resolving a storm spell to generate massive value, direct damage, or token generation
Replaying a lethal finisher or key instant using Ral, Monsoon Mage's enter-the-battlefield ability
Overwhelming the board with storm copies and pumped creatures to bypass interaction

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two cheap instants or sorceries
Hands containing Ruby Medallion or Pyretic Ritual
Hands with Ral, Monsoon Mage and a red/blue instant to replay
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably with fetch/land drop synergy), Ruby Medallion, Pyretic Ritual, and at least one cantrip or win condition.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking mana acceleration or storm enablers
Hands with only high-cost permanents or lands
Hands without instant-speed interaction or card draw
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, no mana acceleration, or only win conditions without pieces to assemble the combo.

examples.reason: Provides immediate acceleration, storm count, and card advantage to dig for combo pieces.
Lacks cheap instants/sorceries to replay or build storm count, making the combo difficult to assemble.

early_game: Play mana rocks and cheap instants to establish acceleration and storm count
Use card draw to dig for combo pieces and finishers
Hold up instant-speed disruption to protect key plays
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or ritual, and drop Ruby Medallion if possible.
Turn 2: Chain rituals and cantrips to draw into your win condition or additional pieces.
Prioritize fetching lands that enter untapped or using mana rocks to maintain tempo.
mid_game: Assemble the storm combo and resolve the finisher
Use Ral, Monsoon Mage to replay discarded or resolved instants for additional value
Apply pressure with pumped creatures if the combo is delayed
Treat your graveyard as a resource for storm count, but avoid unnecessary discards.
Sequence rituals to maximize mana while preserving cards for the storm turn.
Keep cantrips ready to dig through your deck if the combo is disrupted.
Avoid committing to the board unless you have a backup win condition or can stabilize.
prioritize drawing into the combo over trading creatures.
late_game: Stabilize with Ral's recursion and board presence if the combo is countered
Use life total and card advantage to outlast opponents
Look for alternate win conditions like token generation or direct damage spells
Cast 10+ spells in a single turn to trigger storm damage or win conditions like Ral, Monsoon Mage.
Sequence rituals first, then cantrips, then the finisher.
Ensure you have enough mana to cast the finisher for lethal.
Go all-in when you have enough mana sources and rituals to cast 10+ spells, or when you can guarantee the finisher resolves.
If you have a backup win condition or can protect the storm turn, commit aggressively.

key_cards.name: Ruby Medallion
key_cards.notes: Reduces red spell costs, enables early rituals, and buffs storm creatures or finishers.

key_cards.name: Pyretic Ritual
key_cards.notes: Provides flexible, instant-speed red mana to cast combo pieces or finishers early.

key_cards.name: Ral, Monsoon Mage
key_cards.notes: Returns key instants/sorceries from the graveyard to your hand, enabling combo reassembly or lethal replays.

sequencing_tips: Play Ruby Medallion before Pyretic Ritual to maximize red discount and pump
Cast cheap instants before drawing to maximize storm count
Use Ral's ETB to replay the most impactful instant or sorcery in your graveyard
Hold up instant-speed interaction to protect combo pieces from counterspells
Ruby Medallion is crucial for reducing red spell costs, so sequence it early.
Pyretic Ritual provides burst mana but can be a target
protect it with cantrips or counters.
Be mindful of the storm mechanic: your graveyard count matters, so avoid unnecessary discards.
Common mistake: over-committing to the board instead of drawing into the combo.
Always count your spells carefully before the storm turn to ensure lethal damage.

matchups.advice: Race or go off turn two/three. Use disruption to delay attackers and prioritize acceleration.
Play around counterspells by splitting mana or using Ral to replay spells. Flood the board with storm copies to overwhelm hand size.
Apply early pressure with pumped creatures and instants. Use card draw to find combo pieces before they stabilize.
Use cantrips to dig through counters, cast spells in response to their counters, and sequence your storm turn to out-value their hand. Bring in additional counters and protection in the sideboard.

Race them with fast mana and early storm attempts. Use counterspells and artifact hate to disrupt their mana base. Don't let them stabilize; pressure them with early threats or combo out before they reach their big spells.

Out-value with better sequencing and sideboard hate. Prioritize graveyard protection, additional win conditions, and hand disruption. Track their storm count and counters, and aim to resolve your finisher first. Bring in more counters and protection to protect your combo.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (especially in control/mirror), removal targeting your win condition or mana rocks, and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents hold up mana for multiple turns, as they likely have a counter or sweep.
Track their graveyard size if they play storm or graveyard-based strategies.

known_lines.name: Normal storm turn
known_lines.mana_requirement: At least two mana, preferably RR, with a reducer on battlefield.
sequence: Resolve a cost reducer before rituals.
Cast rituals and Manamorphose to grow mana and storm.
Use impulse draw to find more rituals or payoff.
Finish with Grapeshot or transformed Ral.
known_lines.notes: Prefer this when a reducer is already on board and the hand has rituals plus draw.

known_lines.name: Past in Flames reload
known_lines.mana_requirement: Enough mana to cast Past in Flames, then flash back at least one ritual.
sequence: Stock the graveyard with rituals and draw spells.
Cast Past in Flames once the first wave stalls.
Flash back rituals before draw spells.
Use the rebuilt storm count to find or cast Grapeshot.
known_lines.notes: This is the strongest reload line when the graveyard contains multiple rituals/cantrips.

known_lines.name: Wish/payoff line
known_lines.mana_requirement: Mana to cast Wish plus the fetched payoff or answer.
sequence: Build mana and storm with reducer plus rituals.
Cast Wish for the missing payoff or protection.
Resolve the fetched finisher or answer before passing priority.
known_lines.notes: Treat Wish as the missing piece when mana is sufficient.

matchup_posture.notes: Against open countermagic, prefer protection, bait, or additional setup unless the line already includes protection.
Against pressure, accept less perfect setup and try to combo before lethal damage arrives.
Against another combo deck, push earlier attempts rather than waiting for a perfect hand.
